manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: kite, horn, strawberry, coat, rope, handgun, gorilla, hotdog, donkey.
Your opponent's team words are: pantyhose, maggot, chipmunk, margarita, cotton, hair, cave, snowplow.
Distractor words are: seesaw, stroller, snowsuit, envelope, jeans, suitcase, hairnet.
The kill words are: lawnmower.

message.content:
.CLUE: CIRCUS
TARGETS: gorilla, donkey, coat, rope, hotdog, horn
message.reasoning:
We need give a single-word clue related to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts. Provide clue and list of target words we want partner to guess. Need to be careful not to hint opponent or distractor words. Choose a clue that maps to multiple of our words. Our words: kite, horn, strawberry, coat, rope, handgun (maybe weapon), gorilla, hotdog, donkey.

We need clue that relates to several. Maybe "fair"?? Fairground: kite, horn (maybe musical), hotdog, rope (maybe carnival games), donkey? Not great.

Maybe "circus": includes gorilla, donkey, coat (clown coat), rope (tightrope), horn (maybe circus music), hotdog (food). Could be many. However "circus" does not contain any listed word. Could opponent have circus? Not listed. Could distractor? No. So clue "CIRCUS" and target list maybe: gorilla, donkey, coat, rope, hotdog, horn? That's six. Might be okay but risk of them guessing opponent's "cave"? Not associated. Should be safe.

Check each target: Gorilla (circus animal), donkey (circus animal), coat (clown coat), rope (tightrope), hotdog (food vendor), horn (maybe circus music). All plausible.

Make sure no opponent word also linked to circus: pantyhose (maybe performers), maggot (no), chipmunk (no), margarita (maybe bar), cotton (maybe tent?), hair (maybe performers). Could cause confusion but clue is ambiguous. But we trust partner.

Alternatively clue "outdoor": kite, rope, coat (weather), strawberry (picnic), donkey (farm), horn (maybe car). Too many.

Kill word: lawnmower. Not relevant.

We should output CLUE: CIRCUS TARGETS: gorilla, donkey, coat, rope, hotdog, horn. Ensure formatting correct
